IIT Bhilai Launches Three-Day Teacher Training Programme to Strengthen Skill-Based Education
articles.full_content:
Teacher Empowerment Focus: IIT Bhilai Inaugurates Intensive Training Programme for 400 Educators
Indian Institute of Technology Bhilai today inaugurated a three-day Teacher Training Programme sponsored by Samagra Shiksha, aimed at strengthening pedagogical practices, promoting skill-oriented education and encouraging value-based learning among school teachers. The programme will train around 400 teachers in eight batches, with each batch undergoing an intensive three-day training at the institute.
The inaugural session was graced by Professor Rajiv Prakash, Director, Indian Institute of Technology Bhilai, who served as the Patron of the programme and Chief Guest. In his address, Professor Prakash underscored the pivotal role of teachers in nation-building and in shaping an enlightened and responsible society. He urged educators to embrace self-reliance, focus on continuous skill development and guide students towards strong character formation rooted in ethical and value-based education.
The programme is being convened by Dr Mehboob Alam and Dr Dhruv Pratap Singh. Addressing the participants, Dr Mehboob Alam described teachers as guiding lights of society who work tirelessly to dispel the darkness of illiteracy and ignorance. Dr Dhruv Pratap Singh highlighted the enduring impact of teachers on students’ lives, citing former President Dr Avul Pakir Jainulabdeen Abdul Kalam as an example of a global personality who consistently acknowledged the contribution of his school teachers in shaping his success.
The inaugural ceremony was also attended by Dr Kuldeep Kumar Kataria, Dean of Academic Affairs, along with Dr Mahavir Sharma, Head of the Department of Physics, and Dr Mayilmurugan, Head of the Department of Chemistry, as well as faculty members and participating teachers. The programme is expected to significantly enhance teaching competencies, encourage innovative classroom practices and reinforce the integration of skill development in school education across the region.
articles.summary: IIT Bhilai Inaugurates Intensive Training Programme for 400 Educators Indian Institute of Technology Bhilai today inaugurated a three-day Teacher Training Programme sponsored by Samagra Shiksha, aimed at strengthening pedagogical practices, promoting skill-oriented education and encouraging value-based learning among school teachers. The programme will train around 400 teachers in eight batches, with each batch undergoing an intensive three-day training at the institute. The inaugural session was graced by Professor Rajiv Prakash, Director, Indian Institute of Technology Bhilai, who served as the Patron of the programme and Chief Guest.
